Legacy reconciliation requires a verified terminal progressing page chain, an exclusive object-id cursor, and a limit from 1 through 500; it reports selected, skipped, remaining, and next cursor state.
Existing body-bearing SourceVersions are preserved and exact retries are no-delta.

Accepting visual similarity alone would have overwritten the user's later UI-era approved icon geometry and transparency with a subtly re-rendered asset.
findings: Reused BC-PR-012 and C12_projection_bilingual_ui as the existing product-runtime presentation owner; no behavior, content-admission, interaction, or layout boundary changed.
Only the three colored inner marker regions changed to white; red/cream/charcoal cards, white lines, shadows, composition, scale, and all exterior alpha values are preserved from the approved icon.
The current ui/index.html, ui/app.js, and ui/styles.css hashes are exactly unchanged from the pre-edit snapshot.

The active service and the built wheel both expose PNG SHA256 65da4baafc76e060cf6248719ac18b0adf06a87b878768b2c6aeb650be08fd51 and ICO SHA256 665c72dc524b6f6f998c24010c93d2dcd7c96e8ba7e693a7783fe4aca0515d9b.
friction_points: Predictive KB preflight could not retrieve because the active index was durably invalidated pending rebuild; no stale or fallback card influenced the work.
The bundled sharp package lacked detect-libc, so the failed conversion attempt produced no ICO; the existing ICO remained intact until a Windows-native seven-size projection succeeded.
